Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(clemens): auto-fix CONDITIONAL HIGH findings + add PHP stack detection

slim-orchestrate changes:

1. CONDITIONAL + HIGH/CRITICAL auto-fix:
   Previously CONDITIONAL was treated like APPROVED — all findings ignored.
   Now: CONDITIONAL with HIGH/CRITICAL findings triggers an auto-fix round
   (does NOT count as retry). CONDITIONAL without HIGH passes as before.
   After 3 attempts, accepts CONDITIONAL gracefully (no HARD STOP).

2. PHP stack detection:
   - Laravel Pint: lint_autofix_cmd = ./vendor/bin/pint,
     lint_check_cmd = ./vendor/bin/pint --test
   - PHPStan/Larastan: typecheck if vendor/bin/phpstan exists
   - Sail-aware test command detection

Triggered by: live orchestration session where Feb 29 leap year bug
(HIGH in slice-03 review) was accepted without fix, and PHP deterministic
gate was entirely skipped despite Laravel Pint being installed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add force update feature with WebSocket real-time progress

Implement online force update functionality that allows users to upgrade
to the latest version through Web API with real-time progress updates.

Features:
- Version check API (GET /api/version)
- WebSocket upgrade endpoint with real-time progress (GET /api/upgrade/ws)
- 10-step upgrade process with detailed logging
- Automatic backup and rollback mechanism
- Architecture detection (x86_64/aarch64)
- Streaming download with progress updates
- Binary verification before restart
- Automatic cleanup of old backups (keep 3 most recent)
- Support for both systemd and exec restart methods

Security improvements:
- WebSocket endpoint protected by Basic Auth
- File integrity validation
- Automatic rollback on failure
- Permission verification

Technical details:
- Added reqwest dependency for HTTP client
- Real-time progress via WebSocket with JSON messages
- Download from GitHub releases (latest tag)
- Backup files with timestamp for traceability
- Progress updates every 5% to avoid spam

Documentation:
- FORCE_UPDATE_PLAN.md: Detailed implementation plan
- UPDATE_FEATURE.md: Usage guide and API documentation
- FIXES_APPLIED.md: Summary of fixes and improvements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix mortgage calculator comparison showing only Loan Amount

The mortgage calculator stores loan term as 'loanTerm' but the calculation
functions were looking for 'loanTermYears'. This caused termMonths to be 0,
resulting in monthlyPayment and totalInterest being 0, which were then
filtered out by ScenarioComparatorTable.

Fixed by supporting both field names in:
- ScenarioAnalysis.tsx: calculateBaselineResults function
- useScenarios.ts: calculateResultsFromInputs function
- SaveToPortfolioButton.tsx: breakEvenMonths calculation

Add test mode with direction flags for trains.py

Supports testing different directions with CLI flags:
- python trains.py                    # Default: Surbiton → Waterloo
- python trains.py --from sur --to wat # Explicit: Surbiton → Waterloo
- python trains.py --reverse          # Reversed: Waterloo → Surbiton
- python trains.py --from wat --to sur # Explicit: Waterloo → Surbiton

Test output shows:
- Route direction
- Number of departures
- Next 3 trains with times, duration, stops, delays
- Full JSON response

Helpful for verifying stop count fix works in both directions.

test: add unit tests for _adaptive_order_clause() priority weighting

Verifies the ORDER BY clause correctly sorts tasks by effective priority
(priority + adaptive_offset) across 9 test scenarios: same-priority boost,
boost overcoming base priority, complex multi-task ordering, NULL coalescing,
equal effective priorities, fallback without column, large offsets, and
zero-offset natural ordering.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix stop count for Waterloo→Surbiton trains

Problem: Was counting ALL subsequent calling points including stops
beyond the destination, showing 13+ stops for a route with ~2-3.

Solution: Pass destination CRS to parse function and count only stops
up to (not including) the destination. Stops counting when destination
is reached.

Fixes incorrect stop counts for reversed journey direction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(compat): add postcss-preset-env for Chromium 83 compatibility

Dlubal desktop app uses Qt 5.15.2 with Chromium 83 which doesn't
support @layer, oklch(), CSS nesting, or dvh units. Added
postcss-preset-env (stage 0, Chrome >= 83) to transpile:
- @layer → specificity rewrite (cascade-layers polyfill)
- oklch() → rgb() hex values
- CSS nesting → flattened selectors
- color-mix() → @supports progressive enhancement with hex fallback
- dvh → vh fallback via @supports not

Simplify to two bus stops for streamlined commute

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(clemens): add cross-slice semantic consistency check to Gate 3

Extends integration-map agent with Phase H) that validates solution
strategies across slice boundaries:

- MODIFY-Chain Analysis: detects missing methods when multiple slices
  modify the same file (e.g. convertToDates() gap)
- Wrapper/Extension Feasibility: flags vague strategies like
  "wrapper or extension" without concrete class/method specification
- Return-Type Consistency: verifies all consumer call-patterns are
  covered by provider slice outputs across the full chain

Also adds Grep to tools (needed for codebase reference checks)
and Semantic Consistency Gaps to output format + blocking rules.

SOTA-aligned: mirrors spec-kit /speckit.analyze cross-artifact
validation pattern (GitHub) and Blueprint2Code confidence evaluation.

commit.message:
Wire anti-hallucination flags and auto-select best model

WhisperTranscriber now passes --beam-size 5, --max-context 0,
--temperature-inc 0.2, --no-speech-thold 0.5, --split-on-word to
whisper-cli. All knobs configurable via WhisperConfig.

Default model path prefers large-v3-turbo-q5_0 if downloaded, falling
back through progressively smaller models. Benchmarks show this
eliminates repetition loops on meeting audio while adding ~12s to a
21-minute transcription.

commit.message:
Add rolling window composites (5g/10g) and momentum signal

Compute 5-game and 10-game rolling composite strength per team with
z-score normalization and Bayesian shrinkage. The momentum signal
(5g - 10g) captures recent form trends and feeds into win probability
via a 0.02 multiplier. Backtester updated to match live scoring.

commit.message:
fix(mac): scarica modelli AI prima di avviare Qt al primo avvio

Su macOS, il caricamento di torch/transformers dentro il processo Qt
causava "Segmentation fault: 11" al primo avvio (modelli non presenti).
Il launcher ora controlla se i modelli sono in cache e, se mancano,
esegue --download-models in un processo separato senza Qt prima di
lanciare l'app normalmente.

commit.message:
infra: clean up stale dev/* branches from completed and superseded failed tasks

Add immediate remote branch deletion to _merge.sh after successful
merge+push. Previously, remote dev/* branches from completed tasks
lingered for 3-7 days until the watchdog's age-based cleanup caught
them, because db_complete_task overwrites the branch name to "merged
to main" (making DB-based cleanup unable to find them).

Also reduce the watchdog's merged-branch cleanup cutoff from 7 days
to 1 day as a tighter fallback for any missed remote deletions.
